free_text: Production cross sections of fission products via
20.9 MeV proton irradiation of 232Th
Vernadsky Institute of Geochemistry and
Analytical Chemistry, Moscow
NS-20M facility, based on the I-2
linear proton accelerator
Two irradiation sessions were carried
out: long irradiation: duration 1 hour;
short irradiation: duration 1 minute.
The reaction products were measured without
chemical separation using three gamma -spectrometers.
Two of these spectrometers were equipped with coaxial
HP Ge detectors with an energy resolution of 1.7 keV
at the 1332 keV gamma-line of 60Co, and the third was
based on a planar HP Ge detector with an energy
resolution of 0.50 keV at the 122 keV gamma-line of
57Co.
Experimental samples were punched using a die from
thorium and copper foils with thicknesses of 0.1 mm
and 0.015 mm, respectively. Sandwich configuration
was used: 232Th (diam. 10.5 mm) + natCu1 (diam. 10.5
mm) + natCu2 (diam. 24 mm). The 10.5 mm diameter
copper foil served as proton beam monitor, while the
24 mm diameter copper foil functioned as a backing
substrate. The thorium and monitoring copper foils
(both diam. 10.5 mm) were precisely aligned and
attached to the larger backing foil using adhesive
tape. The masses of the thorium and copper foils used
in the first irradiation were 108.7 mg and 10.5 mg,
respectively, and in the second irradiation - 99.1 mg
and 9.5 mg, respectively.
Cross section error includes the
uncertainty of the reaction rate definition only,
which contains the statistical uncertainty of gamma
peaks, the uncertainty of quantum yield, uncertainty
of gamma quanta self-absorption in a sample material,
uncertainty of absolute detection efficiency of gamma
spectrometer.

Entry was approved by Dr. Yu.E.Titarenko.
